116 | class SecurityKernel:
 117 |     SENSITIVE_PATTERNS = [
 118 |         re.compile(r"-----BEGIN[A-Z0-9 ]+KEY-----.*?-----END[A-Z0-9 ]+KEY-----", re.DOTALL),
 119 |         re.compile(r"-----BEGIN CERTIFICATE-----.*?-----END CERTIFICATE-----", re.DOTALL),
 120 |         re.compile(r"(api_key|secret_key|auth_token)\s*[:=]\s*['\"][A-Za-z0-9+/=]{20,}['\"]", re.IGNORECASE),
 121 |         re.compile(r"(password|passwd|pwd)\s*[:=]\s*['\"][^'\"]{8,}['\"]", re.IGNORECASE),
 122 |     ]
 123 | 
 124 |     @staticmethod
 125 |     def compute_file_fingerprint(filepath: pathlib.Path) -> FileFingerprint:
 126 |         try:
 127 |             sha1_hash = hashlib.sha1()
 128 |             with open(filepath, 'rb') as f:
 129 |                 while True:
 130 |                     chunk = f.read(8192)
 131 |                     if not chunk:
 132 |                         break
 133 |                     sha1_hash.update(chunk)
 134 |             
 135 |             sha1_str = sha1_hash.hexdigest()[:8]
 136 |             mtime = os.path.getmtime(filepath)
 137 |             mtime_iso = datetime.datetime.fromtimestamp(mtime).isoformat()
 138 |             size_bytes = filepath.stat().st_size
 139 |             
 140 |             return {"sha1": sha1_str, "mtime_iso": mtime_iso, "size_bytes": size_bytes}
 141 |         except Exception as e:
 142 |             logger.warning(f"Cannot compute fingerprint for {filepath}: {e}")
 143 |             return {"sha1": "unknown", "mtime_iso": "unknown", "size_bytes": 0}
 144 | 
 145 |     @staticmethod
 146 |     def is_binary(filepath: str, ignore_exts: List[str], scan_bytes: int = 2048) -> bool:
 147 |         if any(filepath.lower().endswith(ext) for ext in ignore_exts):
 148 |             return True
 149 |         try:
 150 |             with open(filepath, 'rb') as handle:
 151 |                 chunk = handle.read(scan_bytes)
 152 |             if not chunk:
 153 |                 return False
 154 |             if b'\x00' in chunk:
 155 |                 return True
 156 |             guess, _ = mimetypes.guess_type(filepath)
 157 |             if guess and not guess.startswith(('text', 'application')):
 158 |                 return True
 159 |             text_chars = bytearray({7, 8, 9, 10, 12, 13, 27} | set(range(0x20, 0x7F)))
 160 |             nontext = sum(byte not in text_chars for byte in chunk)
 161 |             return (nontext / len(chunk)) > 0.40
 162 |         except Exception:
 163 |             return True
 164 | 
 165 |     @staticmethod
 166 |     def calculate_entropy(text: str) -> float:
 167 |         if not text:
 168 |             return 0.0
 169 |         entropy = 0.0
 170 |         for idx in range(256):
 171 |             p_x = float(text.count(chr(idx))) / len(text)
 172 |             if p_x > 0:
 173 |                 entropy -= p_x * math.log(p_x, 2)
 174 |         return entropy
 175 | 
 176 |     @classmethod
 177 |     def sanitize_content(cls, content: str) -> str:
 178 |         for pattern in cls.SENSITIVE_PATTERNS:
 179 |             try:
 180 |                 content = pattern.sub("[REDACTED_SENSITIVE_PATTERN]", content)
 181 |             except Exception as e:
 182 |                 logger.debug(f"Regex pattern error during sanitization: {e}")
 183 | 
 184 |         sanitized_lines: List[str] = []
 185 |         for line in content.splitlines():
 186 |             if any(key in line.lower() for key in ['api', 'key', 'secret', 'token', 'auth', 'password']):
 187 |                 if cls.calculate_entropy(line) > 4.5:
 188 |                     parts = line.split('=', 1)
 189 |                     prefix = parts[0] if len(parts) == 2 else line.split(':', 1)[0]
 190 |                     sanitized_lines.append(f"{prefix}= [REDACTED_HIGH_ENTROPY]")
 191 |                     continue
 192 |             sanitized_lines.append(line)
 193 |         return "\n".join(sanitized_lines)

 207 |     def _cache_key(self, file_path: pathlib.Path, content: str) -> str:

slices.name: ASTSliceExtractor
slices.code:
282 | class ASTSliceExtractor(ast.NodeVisitor):
 283 |     def __init__(self, source_code: str, filename: str) -> None:
 284 |         self.source_lines = source_code.splitlines()
 285 |         self.filename = filename.replace("\\", "/")
 286 |         self.slices: List[Dict[str, Any]] = []
 287 |         self.call_graph: List[str] = []
 288 |         self.uncertainties: List[Dict[str, str]] = []
 289 | 
 290 |     def _canonical_slice_id(self, node_name: str, node: ast.AST) -> str:
 291 |         """Stable Slice Identity: Uses structural hashing of the AST body to bypass formatting changes."""
 292 |         try:
 293 |             node_dump = ast.dump(node, annotate_fields=False, include_attributes=False)
 294 |             clean_dump = node_dump.replace(" ", "").replace("\n", "")
 295 |             body_hash = hashlib.sha1(clean_dump.encode("utf-8")).hexdigest()[:10]
 296 |         except Exception:
 297 |             body_hash = "unknown"
 298 |         return f"{self.filename}::{node_name}@{body_hash}"
 299 | 
 300 |     def _expr_to_text(self, node: Optional[ast.AST]) -> str:
 301 |         if node is None: return "unknown"
 302 |         try: return ast.unparse(node)
 303 |         except Exception: return getattr(node, "id", "unknown")
 304 | 
 305 |     def _extract_args(self, node: ast.AST) -> List[str]:
 306 |         if not isinstance(node, (ast.FunctionDef, ast.AsyncFunctionDef)): return []
 307 |         args: List[str] = []
 308 |         for arg in node.args.posonlyargs + node.args.args + node.args.kwonlyargs:
 309 |             args.append(arg.arg)
 310 |         if node.args.vararg: args.append(f"*{node.args.vararg.arg}")
 311 |         if node.args.kwarg: args.append(f"**{node.args.kwarg.arg}")
 312 |         return args
 313 | 
 314 |     def _infer_return_type(self, node: ast.AST) -> str:
 315 |         if isinstance(node, (ast.FunctionDef, ast.AsyncFunctionDef)) and node.returns is not None:
 316 |             return self._expr_to_text(node.returns)
 317 |         return "unknown"
 318 | 
 319 |     def _extract_mutations(self, node: ast.AST) -> List[str]:
 320 |         mutations: Set[str] = set()
 321 |         for child in ast.walk(node):
 322 |             if isinstance(child, ast.Assign):
 323 |                 for t in child.targets:
 324 |                     if isinstance(t, (ast.Name, ast.Attribute, ast.Subscript)):
 325 |                         mutations.add(self._expr_to_text(t))
 326 |             elif isinstance(child, (ast.AnnAssign, ast.AugAssign)):
 327 |                 if isinstance(child.target, (ast.Name, ast.Attribute, ast.Subscript)):
 328 |                     mutations.add(self._expr_to_text(child.target))
 329 |         return sorted(mutations)
 330 | 
 331 |     def _extract_side_effects(self, node: ast.AST, mutations: List[str], calls: List[str]) -> List[str]:
 332 |         effects: Set[str] = set()
 333 |         lowered_calls = {str(c).split('::')[-1].lower() for c in calls} # handle prefixed calls
 334 |         if mutations: effects.add("state_mutation")
 335 |         if lowered_calls & {"open", "write", "dump", "save", "tofile"}: effects.add("file_io")
 336 |         if lowered_calls & {"print", "logger", "info", "warning", "error", "debug"}: effects.add("logging")
 337 |         if lowered_calls & {"request", "get", "post", "fetch", "session"}: effects.add("network_io")
 338 |         if lowered_calls & {"execute", "commit", "query", "session", "add", "flush"}: effects.add("database_io")
 339 |         if lowered_calls & {"cuda", "cupy", "torch", "tensor", "device"}: effects.add("hw_acceleration")
 340 |         return sorted(effects) if effects else ["none"]
 341 | 
 342 |     def _classify_component(self, node_name: str) -> Dict[str, str]:
 343 |         """Slice Role Confidence Classifier."""
 344 |         token = node_name.lower()
 345 |         mapping = {
 346 |             "testing": ["test", "mock", "fixture"],
 347 |             "data_model": ["model", "dto", "schema", "entity"],
 348 |             "service_orchestrator": ["service", "handler", "manager", "dispatch"],
 349 |             "utility": ["util", "helper", "common"],
 350 |             "ui_component": ["view", "ui", "component", "render"],
 351 |             "api_controller": ["api", "route", "controller", "endpoint"],
 352 |             "repository": ["repo", "dao", "db"],
 353 |             "auditor_guard": ["audit", "verify", "check", "guard"],
 354 |             "logic_engine": ["logic", "reason", "compute"]
 355 |         }
 356 |         best_match = "general_component"
 357 |         max_hits = 0
 358 |         
 359 |         for c_type, keywords in mapping.items():
 360 |             hits = sum(1 for k in keywords if k in token)
 361 |             if hits > max_hits:
 362 |                 max_hits = hits
 363 |                 best_match = c_type
 364 |                 
 365 |         confidence = "HIGH" if max_hits >= 2 else "MEDIUM" if max_hits == 1 else "LOW"
 366 |         return {"type": best_match, "confidence": confidence}
 367 | 
 368 |     def _extract_code(self, node: ast.AST) -> str:
 369 |         try:
 370 |             start = max(0, int(getattr(node, "lineno", 1)) - 1)
 371 |             end = min(len(self.source_lines), int(getattr(node, "end_lineno", getattr(node, "lineno", 1))))
 372 |             return "\n".join(f"{start + i + 1:4d} | {line}" for i, line in enumerate(self.source_lines[start:end]))
 373 |         except Exception:
 374 |             return "[ERROR: Could not extract code]"
 375 | 
 376 |     def _process_slice(self, node: ast.AST, slice_type: str) -> None:
 377 |         try:
 378 |             cv = CallVisitor(filename=self.filename)
 379 |             cv.visit(node)
 380 |             node_name = getattr(node, 'name', '<unknown>')
 381 |             calls = sorted(set(cv.calls))
 382 |             mutations = self._extract_mutations(node)
 383 |             
 384 |             # Uncertainty Tracking (eval, exec, getattr, runtime imports)
 385 |             lowered_calls = {str(c).split('::')[-1].lower() for c in calls}
 386 |             if lowered_calls & {"eval", "exec", "getattr", "setattr", "import_module", "__import__"}:
 387 |                 self.uncertainties.append({
 388 |                     "type": "dynamic_behavior",
 389 |                     "slice": str(node_name),
 390 |                     "detail": "eval/exec/getattr/import"
 391 |                 })
 392 |             
 393 |             # Semantic Density Mapping
 394 |             loc = getattr(node, "end_lineno", getattr(node, "lineno", 0)) - getattr(node, "lineno", 0) + 1
 395 |             raw_nodes = len(list(ast.walk(node)))
 396 |             cyclomatic_proxy = int(math.log(raw_nodes + 1) * 10) if raw_nodes > 0 else 0
 397 |             density_score_raw = loc + len(calls) * 2 + len(mutations) * 3 + cyclomatic_proxy
 398 |             density_level = "HIGH" if density_score_raw > 100 else "MEDIUM" if density_score_raw > 40 else "LOW"
 399 | 
 400 |             self.slices.append({
 401 |                 "slice_id": self._canonical_slice_id(str(node_name), node),
 402 |                 "type": slice_type,
 403 |                 "name": node_name,
 404 |                 "start_line": getattr(node, "lineno", 0),
 405 |                 "end_line": getattr(node, "end_lineno", getattr(node, "lineno", 0)),
 406 |                 "code": self._extract_code(node),
 407 |                 "calls": calls,
 408 |                 "signature": {
 409 |                     "args": self._extract_args(node),
 410 |                     "returns": self._infer_return_type(node),
 411 |                     "calls": calls,
 412 |                     "side_effects": self._extract_side_effects(node, mutations, calls),
 413 |                 },
 414 |                 "mutations": mutations,
 415 |                 "component": self._classify_component(str(node_name)),
 416 |                 "density": {
 417 |                     "loc": loc,
 418 |                     "cyclomatic_proxy": cyclomatic_proxy,
 419 |                     "level": density_level,
 420 |                     "score": density_score_raw
 421 |                 }
 422 |             })
 423 |         except Exception as e:
 424 |             logger.debug(f"Error processing {slice_type} slice: {e}")
 425 |         finally:
 426 |             self.generic_visit(node)

290 |     def _canonical_slice_id(self, node_name: str, node: ast.AST) -> str:
 291 |         """Stable Slice Identity: Uses structural hashing of the AST body to bypass formatting changes."""
 292 |         try:
 293 |             node_dump = ast.dump(node, annotate_fields=False, include_attributes=False)
 294 |             clean_dump = node_dump.replace(" ", "").replace("\n", "")

342 |     def _classify_component(self, node_name: str) -> Dict[str, str]:
 343 |         """Slice Role Confidence Classifier."""
 344 |         token = node_name.lower()
 345 |         mapping = {
 346 |             "testing": ["test", "mock", "fixture"],
 347 |             "data_model": ["model", "dto", "schema", "entity"],
 348 |             "service_orchestrator": ["service", "handler", "manager", "dispatch"],
 349 |             "utility": ["util", "helper", "common"],
 350 |             "ui_component": ["view", "ui", "component", "render"],
 351 |             "api_controller": ["api", "route", "controller", "endpoint"],
 352 |             "repository": ["repo", "dao", "db"],
 353 |             "auditor_guard": ["audit", "verify", "check", "guard"],
 354 |             "logic_engine": ["logic", "reason", "compute"]
 355 |         }
 356 |         best_match = "general_component"
 357 |         max_hits = 0
 358 |         
 359 |         for c_type, keywords in mapping.items():
 360 |             hits = sum(1 for k in keywords if k in token)
 361 |             if hits > max_hits:
 362 |                 max_hits = hits
 363 |                 best_match = c_type
 364 |                 
 365 |         confidence = "HIGH" if max_hits >= 2 else "MEDIUM" if max_hits == 1 else "LOW"
 366 |         return {"type": best_match, "confidence": confidence}

slices.name: _process_slice
slices.code:
376 |     def _process_slice(self, node: ast.AST, slice_type: str) -> None:
 377 |         try:
 378 |             cv = CallVisitor(filename=self.filename)
 379 |             cv.visit(node)
 380 |             node_name = getattr(node, 'name', '<unknown>')
 381 |             calls = sorted(set(cv.calls))
 382 |             mutations = self._extract_mutations(node)
 383 |             
 384 |             # Uncertainty Tracking (eval, exec, getattr, runtime imports)
 385 |             lowered_calls = {str(c).split('::')[-1].lower() for c in calls}
 386 |             if lowered_calls & {"eval", "exec", "getattr", "setattr", "import_module", "__import__"}:
 387 |                 self.uncertainties.append({
 388 |                     "type": "dynamic_behavior",
 389 |                     "slice": str(node_name),
 390 |                     "detail": "eval/exec/getattr/import"
 391 |                 })
 392 |             
 393 |             # Semantic Density Mapping
 394 |             loc = getattr(node, "end_lineno", getattr(node, "lineno", 0)) - getattr(node, "lineno", 0) + 1
 395 |             raw_nodes = len(list(ast.walk(node)))
 396 |             cyclomatic_proxy = int(math.log(raw_nodes + 1) * 10) if raw_nodes > 0 else 0
 397 |             density_score_raw = loc + len(calls) * 2 + len(mutations) * 3 + cyclomatic_proxy
 398 |             density_level = "HIGH" if density_score_raw > 100 else "MEDIUM" if density_score_raw > 40 else "LOW"
 399 | 
 400 |             self.slices.append({
 401 |                 "slice_id": self._canonical_slice_id(str(node_name), node),
 402 |                 "type": slice_type,
 403 |                 "name": node_name,
 404 |                 "start_line": getattr(node, "lineno", 0),
 405 |                 "end_line": getattr(node, "end_lineno", getattr(node, "lineno", 0)),
 406 |                 "code": self._extract_code(node),
 407 |                 "calls": calls,
 408 |                 "signature": {
 409 |                     "args": self._extract_args(node),
 410 |                     "returns": self._infer_return_type(node),
 411 |                     "calls": calls,
 412 |                     "side_effects": self._extract_side_effects(node, mutations, calls),
 413 |                 },
 414 |                 "mutations": mutations,
 415 |                 "component": self._classify_component(str(node_name)),
 416 |                 "density": {
 417 |                     "loc": loc,
 418 |                     "cyclomatic_proxy": cyclomatic_proxy,
 419 |                     "level": density_level,
 420 |                     "score": density_score_raw
 421 |                 }
 422 |             })
 423 |         except Exception as e:
 424 |             logger.debug(f"Error processing {slice_type} slice: {e}")
 425 |         finally:
 426 |             self.generic_visit(node)

 662 |         for f in self.files_registry:

 669 |                 
 670 |                 muts_count = len(s.get("mutations", []))
 671 |                 effects = s.get("signature", {}).get("side_effects", [])

 673 |                 effect_weight = 0

 677 |                     elif e == "state_mutation":

 679 |                     elif e == "logging":

 687 |                 risk_meta[sid] = {
 688 |                     "slice_id": sid,

 696 |                     "side_effects_weight": effect_weight,
 697 |                     "entrypoint_context": is_entry,
 698 |                     "risk_score": score,

 746 |     def _build_execution_paths(self, slice_graph: Dict[str, List[str]]) -> Dict[str, Dict[str, Any]]:
 747 |         """Execution Flow Confidence mapping + Truncation Tracking."""
 748 |         file_entry_slices: Dict[str, Dict[str, Any]] = {}
 749 |         for file_meta in self.files_registry:
 750 |             summary = file_meta.get("summary") or {}
 751 |             if not summary.get("is_entry_point"): continue
 752 |             
 753 |             entry_candidates: List[str] = []
 754 |             confidence = "HIGH"
 755 |             
 756 |             for s in summary.get("slices", []):
 757 |                 if str(s.get("name", "")).lower() in {"main", "run", "start", "cli", "entrypoint"}:
 758 |                     entry_candidates.append(s.get("slice_id"))
 759 |                     
 760 |             if not entry_candidates:
 761 |                 confidence = "MEDIUM"
 762 |                 entry_candidates = [s.get("slice_id") for s in summary.get("slices", []) if s.get("type") in {"function", "async_function"}]
 763 |                 
 764 |             uncerts = summary.get("uncertainties", [])
 765 |             dynamic_count = sum(1 for u in uncerts if isinstance(u, dict) and u.get("type") == "dynamic_behavior")
 766 |             if dynamic_count >= 2:
 767 |                 confidence = "LOW"
 768 |             elif dynamic_count == 1 and confidence == "HIGH":
 769 |                 confidence = "MEDIUM"
 770 |                 
 771 |             if entry_candidates:
 772 |                 file_entry_slices[file_meta.get("path", "unknown")] = {
 773 |                     "roots": [sid for sid in entry_candidates if sid], 
 774 |                     "confidence": confidence
 775 |                 }
 776 | 
 777 |         execution_paths: Dict[str, Dict[str, Any]] = {}
 778 |         for file_path, entry_info in file_entry_slices.items():
 779 |             discovered: List[str] = []
 780 |             visited: Set[str] = set()
 781 |             frontier = list(entry_info["roots"])
 782 |             depth = 0
 783 |             while frontier and depth < 6: # max depth
 784 |                 next_frontier: List[str] = []
 785 |                 for node in frontier:
 786 |                     if node in visited: continue
 787 |                     visited.add(node)
 788 |                     discovered.append(node)
 789 |                     for child in slice_graph.get(node, []):
 790 |                         if child not in visited: next_frontier.append(child)
 791 |                 frontier = next_frontier
 792 |                 depth += 1
 793 |             execution_paths[file_path] = {
 794 |                 "path": discovered,
 795 |                 "confidence": entry_info["confidence"],
 796 |                 "truncated": depth >= 6
 797 |             }
 798 | 
 799 |         return execution_paths

 801 |     def _extract_system_architecture_context(self) -> Dict[str, Any]:
 802 |         contracts: List[str] = []
 803 |         observability: List[str] = []
 804 |         failure_conds: List[str] = []
 805 |         components: Dict[str, Dict[str, Any]] = {}
 806 |         external_modules: Dict[str, Set[str]] = {"stdlib": set(), "third_party": set()}
 807 | 
 808 |         assert_pattern = re.compile(r"\bassert\s+(.+)")
 809 |         raise_pattern = re.compile(r"\braise\s+([A-Za-z]+(?:Error|Exception))\b")
 810 |         log_pattern = re.compile(r"\b(?:logger|logging|log|metrics|stats)\.(info|warning|error|debug|critical)\b(.+)")
 811 |         stdlib_names = getattr(sys, 'stdlib_module_names', set(['os', 'sys', 're', 'math', 'json', 'datetime', 'hashlib', 'pathlib', 'logging', 'subprocess', 'ast', 'concurrent', 'csv', 'argparse', 'typing', 'collections', 'itertools', 'functools']))
 812 | 
 813 |         internal_paths = {f['path'].replace('.py', '').replace('/', '.') for f in self.files_registry}
 814 |         internal_bases = {p.split('.')[0] for p in internal_paths}
 815 | 
 816 |         for file_meta in self.files_registry:
 817 |             path = file_meta.get("path", "unknown")
 818 |             content = file_meta.get("content", "")
 819 |             summary = file_meta.get("summary") or {}
 820 |             
 821 |             # External Module Tracking (Granular Classification)
 822 |             for imp in summary.get("import_graph", []):
 823 |                 base_mod = imp.split('.')[0]
 824 |                 if base_mod not in internal_bases:
 825 |                     if base_mod in stdlib_names:
 826 |                         external_modules["stdlib"].add(imp)
 827 |                     else:
 828 |                         external_modules["third_party"].add(imp)
 829 | 
 830 |             for slice_meta in summary.get("slices", []):
 831 |                 comp = slice_meta.get("component", {})
 832 |                 comp_type = comp.get("type", "general_component")
 833 |                 if comp_type != "general_component":
 834 |                     components[f"{path}::{slice_meta.get('name')}"] = {
 835 |                         "type": comp_type, 
 836 |                         "slice_id": slice_meta.get("slice_id"),
 837 |                         "confidence": comp.get("confidence", "LOW")
 838 |                     }
 839 | 
 840 |             for line in content.splitlines():
 841 |                 line = line.strip()
 842 |                 if not line or line.startswith('#'): continue
 843 |                 
 844 |                 # Contracts
 845 |                 if m := assert_pattern.search(line): contracts.append(f"Assertion constraint: {m.group(1)[:50]}")
 846 |                 elif m := raise_pattern.search(line): failure_conds.append(f"Throws {m.group(1)}: {line[:50]}")
 847 |                 
 848 |                 # Observability
 849 |                 if m := log_pattern.search(line): observability.append(f"{m.group(1).upper()} Log trigger: {line[:60]}")
 850 | 
 851 |         def dedupe(items: List[str], limit=30) -> List[str]:
 852 |             seen, out = set(), []
 853 |             for x in items:
 854 |                 if x not in seen:
 855 |                     seen.add(x)
 856 |                     out.append(x)
 857 |                     if len(out) >= limit: break
 858 |             return out
 859 | 
 860 |         return {
 861 |             "system_contracts": dedupe(contracts),
 862 |             "observability_telemetry": dedupe(observability),
 863 |             "failure_conditions": dedupe(failure_conds),
 864 |             "external_dependencies": {
 865 |                 "stdlib": sorted(external_modules["stdlib"]),
 866 |                 "third_party": sorted(external_modules["third_party"])
 867 |             },
 868 |             "component_registry": components,
 869 |         }

 873 |         motifs = []

 882 |                     stateful_io_handlers.append(s.get("slice_id"))

 886 |

 888 |             motifs.append({

 897 |                 "slices": pure_compute,

1321 |     def run(self, format_type: str = "text", heatmap: bool = False, explain: Optional[str] = None) -> str:
1322 |         logger.info(f"Scanning {len(self.target_files)} targeted files...")
1323 |         analyzer = PolyglotAnalyzer(event_callback=self.event_callback)
1324 | 
1325 |         with ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=self.workers) as executor:
1326 |             future_to_path = {executor.submit(self._process_single_file, p, analyzer): p for p in self.target_files}
1327 |             for idx, future in enumerate(as_completed(future_to_path), start=1):
1328 |                 try:
1329 |                     res = future.result()
1330 |                     if res.get("status") == "bundled":
1331 |                         self.files_registry.append(res["entry"])
1332 |                         self.scan_stats["bundled"] += 1
1333 |                         if res.get("syntax_error"): self.syntax_errors.append(res["syntax_error"])
1334 |                     elif res.get("status") == "skipped":
1335 |                         self._track_skip(res.get("skip_reason", "binary_or_ext"), res.get("ext", ""))
1336 |                     else:
1337 |                         self.scan_stats["errors"] += 1
1338 |                 except Exception:
1339 |                     self.scan_stats["errors"] += 1
1340 | 
1341 |         self.files_registry.sort(key=lambda x: (x.get('complexity', 0), x.get('path', '')))
1342 |         
1343 |         logger.info(f"Scan complete - Bundled: {self.scan_stats['bundled']}, Skipped: {self.scan_stats['skipped']}, Errors: {self.scan_stats['errors']}")
1344 |         
1345 |         # Build graphs for diagnostics
1346 |         if heatmap or explain:
1347 |             slice_graph = self._build_slice_dependency_graph()
1348 |             reverse_graph = self._build_reverse_dependency_graph(slice_graph)
1349 |             risk_meta = self._compute_slice_risk_scores(slice_graph, reverse_graph)
1350 | 
1351 |             if heatmap: self.print_heatmap(risk_meta)
1352 |             if explain: self.print_explanation(explain, risk_meta)
1353 | 
1354 |         return self._generate_json_output() if format_type == "json" else self._generate_text_output()

563 |     def _process_single_file(self, path_obj: pathlib.Path, analyzer: "PolyglotAnalyzer") -> Dict[str, Any]:
 564 |         try:
 565 |             if not path_obj.exists() or not path_obj.is_file(): return {"status": "error"}
 566 |             if SecurityKernel.is_binary(str(path_obj), self.ignore_exts): return {"status": "skipped", "skip_reason": "binary_or_ext", "ext": path_obj.suffix}
 567 |             
 568 |             size = path_obj.stat().st_size
 569 |             if size > MAX_FILE_SIZE_BYTES: return {"status": "skipped", "skip_reason": "oversize", "ext": path_obj.suffix}
 570 | 
 571 |             with open(path_obj, "r", encoding="utf-8", errors="ignore") as handle:
 572 |                 raw = handle.read()
 573 | 
 574 |             if self._looks_like_formatter_output(raw):
 575 |                 return {"status": "skipped", "skip_reason": "recursive_bundle", "ext": path_obj.suffix}
 576 | 
 577 |             try: rel_path = path_obj.relative_to(self.base_path).as_posix()
 578 |             except ValueError: return {"status": "skipped", "skip_reason": "outside_base", "ext": path_obj.suffix}
 579 | 
 580 |             # AST Analysis
 581 |             cached = self.ast_cache.get(path_obj, raw) if path_obj.suffix == '.py' else None
 582 |             analysis = cached or analyzer.analyze(rel_path, raw)
 583 |             if not cached and path_obj.suffix == '.py': self.ast_cache.set(path_obj, raw, analysis)
 584 | 
 585 |             summary = analysis.get("summary") or {}
 586 |             syntax_error = f"{rel_path} (Line {summary.get('error_line', '?')}): {summary.get('error')}" if not summary.get("syntax_valid", True) else None
 587 | 
 588 |             return {
 589 |                 "status": "bundled",
 590 |                 "entry": {
 591 |                     "path": rel_path,
 592 |                     "size_bytes": size,
 593 |                     "content": raw,
 594 |                     "summary": summary,
 595 |                     "complexity": analysis.get("complexity", 0),
 596 |                     "fingerprint": SecurityKernel.compute_file_fingerprint(path_obj),
 597 |                 },
 598 |                 "syntax_error": syntax_error,
 599 |             }
 600 |         except Exception:
 601 |             return {"status": "error"}

 628 |         name_to_slice_ids: Dict[str, List[str]] = {}
 629 |         for f in self.files_registry:

 631 |                 name = str(s.get("name", "")).lower()

 635 |                     name_to_slice_ids.setdefault(name, []).append(s.get("slice_id"))
 636 |                     name_to_slice_ids.setdefault(fq_name, []).append(s.get("slice_id"))

 639 |         for f in self.files_registry:

 641 |                 sid = s.get("slice_id")
 642 |                 targets = set()
 643 |                 for called in s.get("calls", []):
 644 |                     search_key = str(called).lower()
 645 |                     for target_sid in name_to_slice_ids.get(search_key, []):
 646 |                         if target_sid != sid: targets.add(target_sid)

659 |     def _compute_slice_risk_scores(self, slice_graph: Dict[str, List[str]], reverse_graph: Dict[str, List[str]]) -> Dict[str, Dict[str, Any]]:
 660 |         """Semantic Risk Model: factors in structural connections, mutations, and system side-effects with log scale saturation."""
 661 |         risk_meta: Dict[str, Dict[str, Any]] = {}
 662 |         for f in self.files_registry:
 663 |             summary = f.get('summary') or {}
 664 |             is_entry = summary.get("is_entry_point", False)
 665 |             for s in summary.get("slices", []):
 666 |                 sid = s.get("slice_id")
 667 |                 callers_count = len(reverse_graph.get(sid, []))
 668 |                 callees_count = len(slice_graph.get(sid, []))
 669 |                 
 670 |                 muts_count = len(s.get("mutations", []))
 671 |                 effects = s.get("signature", {}).get("side_effects", [])
 672 |                 
 673 |                 effect_weight = 0
 674 |                 for e in effects:
 675 |                     if e in {"file_io", "network_io", "database_io", "hw_acceleration"}:
 676 |                         effect_weight += 15
 677 |                     elif e == "state_mutation":
 678 |                         effect_weight += 5
 679 |                     elif e == "logging":
 680 |                         effect_weight += 2
 681 |                 
 682 |                 raw_score = callers_count * 20 + callees_count * 10 + muts_count * 5 + effect_weight + (20 if is_entry else 0)
 683 |                 # Soft scaling to prevent harsh clumping at 100
 684 |                 score = int(100 * (1 - math.exp(-raw_score / 100.0)))
 685 |                 
 686 |                 level = "CRITICAL" if score >= 80 else "HIGH" if score >= 60 else "MEDIUM" if score >= 40 else "LOW"
 687 |                 risk_meta[sid] = {
 688 |                     "slice_id": sid,
 689 |                     "file_path": f.get("path", "unknown"),
 690 |                     "slice_name": s.get("name", "unknown"),
 691 |                     "callers": reverse_graph.get(sid, []),
 692 |                     "callees": slice_graph.get(sid, []),
 693 |                     "callers_count": callers_count,
 694 |                     "callees_count": callees_count,
 695 |                     "mutations_count": muts_count,
 696 |                     "side_effects_weight": effect_weight,
 697 |                     "entrypoint_context": is_entry,
 698 |                     "risk_score": score,
 699 |                     "risk_level": level,
 700 |                 }
 701 |         return risk_meta

746 |     def _build_execution_paths(self, slice_graph: Dict[str, List[str]]) -> Dict[str, Dict[str, Any]]:
 747 |         """Execution Flow Confidence mapping + Truncation Tracking."""
 748 |         file_entry_slices: Dict[str, Dict[str, Any]] = {}
 749 |         for file_meta in self.files_registry:
 750 |             summary = file_meta.get("summary") or {}
 751 |             if not summary.get("is_entry_point"): continue
 752 |             
 753 |             entry_candidates: List[str] = []
 754 |             confidence = "HIGH"
 755 |             
 756 |             for s in summary.get("slices", []):
 757 |                 if str(s.get("name", "")).lower() in {"main", "run", "start", "cli", "entrypoint"}:
 758 |                     entry_candidates.append(s.get("slice_id"))
 759 |                     
 760 |             if not entry_candidates:
 761 |                 confidence = "MEDIUM"
 762 |                 entry_candidates = [s.get("slice_id") for s in summary.get("slices", []) if s.get("type") in {"function", "async_function"}]
 763 |                 
 764 |             uncerts = summary.get("uncertainties", [])
 765 |             dynamic_count = sum(1 for u in uncerts if isinstance(u, dict) and u.get("type") == "dynamic_behavior")
 766 |             if dynamic_count >= 2:
 767 |                 confidence = "LOW"
 768 |             elif dynamic_count == 1 and confidence == "HIGH":
 769 |                 confidence = "MEDIUM"
 770 |                 
 771 |             if entry_candidates:
 772 |                 file_entry_slices[file_meta.get("path", "unknown")] = {
 773 |                     "roots": [sid for sid in entry_candidates if sid], 
 774 |                     "confidence": confidence
 775 |                 }
 776 | 
 777 |         execution_paths: Dict[str, Dict[str, Any]] = {}
 778 |         for file_path, entry_info in file_entry_slices.items():
 779 |             discovered: List[str] = []
 780 |             visited: Set[str] = set()
 781 |             frontier = list(entry_info["roots"])
 782 |             depth = 0
 783 |             while frontier and depth < 6: # max depth
 784 |                 next_frontier: List[str] = []
 785 |                 for node in frontier:
 786 |                     if node in visited: continue
 787 |                     visited.add(node)
 788 |                     discovered.append(node)
 789 |                     for child in slice_graph.get(node, []):
 790 |                         if child not in visited: next_frontier.append(child)
 791 |                 frontier = next_frontier
 792 |                 depth += 1
 793 |             execution_paths[file_path] = {
 794 |                 "path": discovered,
 795 |                 "confidence": entry_info["confidence"],
 796 |                 "truncated": depth >= 6
 797 |             }
 798 | 
 799 |         return execution_paths

801 |     def _extract_system_architecture_context(self) -> Dict[str, Any]:
 802 |         contracts: List[str] = []
 803 |         observability: List[str] = []
 804 |         failure_conds: List[str] = []
 805 |         components: Dict[str, Dict[str, Any]] = {}
 806 |         external_modules: Dict[str, Set[str]] = {"stdlib": set(), "third_party": set()}
 807 | 
 808 |         assert_pattern = re.compile(r"\bassert\s+(.+)")
 809 |         raise_pattern = re.compile(r"\braise\s+([A-Za-z]+(?:Error|Exception))\b")
 810 |         log_pattern = re.compile(r"\b(?:logger|logging|log|metrics|stats)\.(info|warning|error|debug|critical)\b(.+)")
 811 |         stdlib_names = getattr(sys, 'stdlib_module_names', set(['os', 'sys', 're', 'math', 'json', 'datetime', 'hashlib', 'pathlib', 'logging', 'subprocess', 'ast', 'concurrent', 'csv', 'argparse', 'typing', 'collections', 'itertools', 'functools']))
 812 | 
 813 |         internal_paths = {f['path'].replace('.py', '').replace('/', '.') for f in self.files_registry}
 814 |         internal_bases = {p.split('.')[0] for p in internal_paths}
 815 | 
 816 |         for file_meta in self.files_registry:
 817 |             path = file_meta.get("path", "unknown")
 818 |             content = file_meta.get("content", "")
 819 |             summary = file_meta.get("summary") or {}
 820 |             
 821 |             # External Module Tracking (Granular Classification)
 822 |             for imp in summary.get("import_graph", []):
 823 |                 base_mod = imp.split('.')[0]
 824 |                 if base_mod not in internal_bases:
 825 |                     if base_mod in stdlib_names:
 826 |                         external_modules["stdlib"].add(imp)
 827 |                     else:
 828 |                         external_modules["third_party"].add(imp)
 829 | 
 830 |             for slice_meta in summary.get("slices", []):
 831 |                 comp = slice_meta.get("component", {})
 832 |                 comp_type = comp.get("type", "general_component")
 833 |                 if comp_type != "general_component":
 834 |                     components[f"{path}::{slice_meta.get('name')}"] = {
 835 |                         "type": comp_type, 
 836 |                         "slice_id": slice_meta.get("slice_id"),
 837 |                         "confidence": comp.get("confidence", "LOW")
 838 |                     }
 839 | 
 840 |             for line in content.splitlines():
 841 |                 line = line.strip()
 842 |                 if not line or line.startswith('#'): continue
 843 |                 
 844 |                 # Contracts
 845 |                 if m := assert_pattern.search(line): contracts.append(f"Assertion constraint: {m.group(1)[:50]}")
 846 |                 elif m := raise_pattern.search(line): failure_conds.append(f"Throws {m.group(1)}: {line[:50]}")
 847 |                 
 848 |                 # Observability
 849 |                 if m := log_pattern.search(line): observability.append(f"{m.group(1).upper()} Log trigger: {line[:60]}")
 850 | 
 851 |         def dedupe(items: List[str], limit=30) -> List[str]:
 852 |             seen, out = set(), []
 853 |             for x in items:
 854 |                 if x not in seen:
 855 |                     seen.add(x)
 856 |                     out.append(x)
 857 |                     if len(out) >= limit: break
 858 |             return out
 859 | 
 860 |         return {
 861 |             "system_contracts": dedupe(contracts),
 862 |             "observability_telemetry": dedupe(observability),
 863 |             "failure_conditions": dedupe(failure_conds),
 864 |             "external_dependencies": {
 865 |                 "stdlib": sorted(external_modules["stdlib"]),
 866 |                 "third_party": sorted(external_modules["third_party"])
 867 |             },
 868 |             "component_registry": components,
 869 |         }

1321 |     def run(self, format_type: str = "text", heatmap: bool = False, explain: Optional[str] = None) -> str:
1322 |         logger.info(f"Scanning {len(self.target_files)} targeted files...")
1323 |         analyzer = PolyglotAnalyzer(event_callback=self.event_callback)
1324 | 
1325 |         with ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=self.workers) as executor:
1326 |             future_to_path = {executor.submit(self._process_single_file, p, analyzer): p for p in self.target_files}
1327 |             for idx, future in enumerate(as_completed(future_to_path), start=1):
1328 |                 try:
1329 |                     res = future.result()
1330 |                     if res.get("status") == "bundled":
1331 |                         self.files_registry.append(res["entry"])
1332 |                         self.scan_stats["bundled"] += 1
1333 |                         if res.get("syntax_error"): self.syntax_errors.append(res["syntax_error"])
1334 |                     elif res.get("status") == "skipped":
1335 |                         self._track_skip(res.get("skip_reason", "binary_or_ext"), res.get("ext", ""))
1336 |                     else:
1337 |                         self.scan_stats["errors"] += 1
1338 |                 except Exception:
1339 |                     self.scan_stats["errors"] += 1
1340 | 
1341 |         self.files_registry.sort(key=lambda x: (x.get('complexity', 0), x.get('path', '')))
1342 |         
1343 |         logger.info(f"Scan complete - Bundled: {self.scan_stats['bundled']}, Skipped: {self.scan_stats['skipped']}, Errors: {self.scan_stats['errors']}")
1344 |         
1345 |         # Build graphs for diagnostics
1346 |         if heatmap or explain:
1347 |             slice_graph = self._build_slice_dependency_graph()
1348 |             reverse_graph = self._build_reverse_dependency_graph(slice_graph)
1349 |             risk_meta = self._compute_slice_risk_scores(slice_graph, reverse_graph)
1350 | 
1351 |             if heatmap: self.print_heatmap(risk_meta)
1352 |             if explain: self.print_explanation(explain, risk_meta)
1353 | 
1354 |         return self._generate_json_output() if format_type == "json" else self._generate_text_output()
